name: Gap-Formalization (理论/算法)
full_intro_template: Paragraph 1 (Context + Why now): In recent years, <field> has become a central component in <application scenario>. As systems are increasingly deployed in <high-stakes setting>, ensuring <target property> is no longer optional but essential.

Paragraph 2 (Precise problem setup): We study the problem of <problem name>. Given <input / distribution / protocol>, the goal is to distinguish or optimize between <case A> and <case B> under <constraint>. Formally, let <notation> denote <object>.

Paragraph 3 (Gap in prior work): Existing approaches mainly rely on <mainstream approach>, which works well when <assumption>. However, these methods become inadequate when <hard condition>. In particular, they suffer from <computational / statistical bottleneck>.

Paragraph 4 (Our key idea): To address this gap, we reformulate <original problem> as <new perspective>. This reformulation enables <algorithmic benefit>, and avoids <previous bottleneck> without requiring <expensive dependency>.

Paragraph 5 (Main results): Our main result shows that <algorithm/theorem> achieves <runtime/bound>. The guarantee is <optimal / near-optimal> in terms of <criterion>. We further extend the result to <variant setting> with <additional guarantee>.
Paragraph 6 (Empirical/theoretical validation): We validate our framework through <experiments / proofs>, showing that it captures <desired phenomenon> and scales to <large regime>.
Paragraph 7 (Contributions list): In summary, our contributions are: (i) <formalization>; (ii) <core method>; (iii) <guarantee>; (iv) <validation and insight>.
abstract_template: P1: Introduce task significance and current bottleneck in one sentence.
P2: State formal problem and your technical angle.
P3: Report main algorithmic/theoretical result with numbers.
P4: Mention validation scope and broader implication.
sentence_stems: We initiate the algorithmic study of ... through the lens of ...
To our knowledge, this is the first work that formalizes ...
Our approach reformulates ... as ..., enabling ... complexity.
